results.title: NASA 2: the American space odyssey
results.creator_credit: Don Wiseman, Lynne Lucius Moore, AV Corporation and United States. National Aeronautics Space Administration
results.brief_description: Part two of a three part series of programs about NASA space flights entitled “The American Space Odyssey”. “Gemini project” is an award winning documentary produced by NASA, introduced by astronaut Charles Duke and narrated by astronaut Bob Marcato, that details the space missions that preceded and laid the groundwork for the Apollo Project. The Gemini project consisted of ten missions carried out in the space of eighteen months and provided data and test results essential to the success of the Apollo missions. “Apollo 16: nothing so hidden” introduced by astronaut Charles Duke and narrated by astronaut Stan Wessel, details the mission to the lunar highlands of the moon which cover 80% of its surface. Astronauts Charlie Duke and John Young scaled mountains in their lunar vehicle to collect samples and conduct experiments. This mission is also memorable for the impressive photos of the earth and space taken with a special camera. “Astro smiles: laughs in space”, introduced by astronaut Wally Schirra takes a look at some of the more light-hearted moments of astronauts experiences in space.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Don Wiseman, Lynne Lucius Moore, AV Corporation and United States. National Aeronautics Space Administration

results.title: NASA 1: an American space odyssey
results.creator_credit: Lester Novros, Don Wiseman, AV Corporation, Graphic Films and United States. National Aeronautics Space Administration
results.brief_description: Part one of a three part series of programs about NASA space flights entitled “The American Space Odyssey”. “NASA 1” traces the history of American space travel championed by United States President John F. Kennedy and contains extensive footage from various space missions including Apollo 7, Apollo 9 and Apollo 13. This documentary comprises archival images of various take-offs, the earth from outer space, the launching of satellites, the effects of zero gravity, astronauts amending equipment whilst hanging in space, and craft re-entry to earth. Astronaut Walter Cunningham describes the events that surrounded the dramatic mission that was Apollo 13’s, whilst other astronauts describe the process of launching a rocket, the emotions, the perils and the joys of space flight, narrating their experiences whilst watching videos of their trips. Also features excerpts from an earlier documentary by Lester Novros (who worked on Kubrick’s “2001: A Space odyssey”), with images of galactic whirlpools, stars, the universe and black holes taken from satellites, both manned and unmanned as well as theories of universal evolution and the wonders of space. Narrated by William Shatner (“Star Trek”).

results.title: Narcotics: why not?
results.brief_description: Sixteen residents of the California Rehabilitation Centre relate how they were introduced to pills, glue, marijuana, and heroin - what it was like and the hopes they hold for the future. These former addicts give telling extemporaneous statements as to what traffic in drugs and narcotics means.

results.title: Narcotics file: the victims
results.creator_credit: Pierre Desbonnet, Journal Films and United Nations Television
results.brief_description: Examines the various treatment programs being attempted throughout the world, admitting with reluctance that as yet there is no cure for heroin addiction. Shows drug-free communes, methadone maintenance, heroin maintenance, harsh jail sentences, one-to-one psychological treatment and prison-treatment centres.

results.title: Narcotics file: the source
results.creator_credit: Pierre Desbonnet, Journal Films and United Nations Television
results.brief_description: Deals with Thailand’s part in the “Golden Triangle” which originates about half the world’s illicit opium supply. Here a joint UN-Thai Government project is attempting to provide alternative cash crops for the hill tribes who earn what little money they have from the cultivation of the opium poppy.

results.title: Nara: a stroll through history
results.creator_credit: Shin Nippon and Shu Taguchi Productions
results.brief_description: The first of Japan’s capitals built in the middle of the sixth century, Nara is one of the country’s most ancient cities. It is a great temple with broad avenues and many fine buildings. The capital was moved to Kyoto, but Nara is still known as the “cradle of Japanese culture”.

results.title: Naqoyqatsi: life as war
results.creator_credit: Godfrey Reggio, Joe Beirne, Lawrence Taub and Miramax Films
results.brief_description: The last of a trilogy that consists of “Koyaanisqatsi” (1982) and “Powaqqatsi” (1988) by Godfrey Reggio. In this instalment Reggio addresses the effect that technology has on our lives and the detrimental impact of advertising, media and technology to create a life out of balance. Music score by Phillip Glass. Director Steven Soderbergh served as executive producer.

results.title: Napoli
results.creator_credit: Kirsten Ralov, Preben Montell, Danmarks Radio and National Video Corporation
results.brief_description: Performed by the Royal Danish Ballet at the Royal Theatre, Copenhagen, “Napoli” is a ballet in three acts by 19th century dancer and choreographer, August Bournonville with music by E. Helsted, G. Rossini, Niels W. Gade, H.S. Paulli, H.C. Lumbye. It is a romantic tale of a young fisherman, Gennaro (Arne Villumsen) and his beloved Teresina (Linda Hindberg) set in the delightful Italian seaside town of Naples. Supporting cast includes Mona Jensen, Flemming Ryberg, Fredbjorn Bjornsson, Tommy Frishoi, Palle Jacobsen with the Royal Danish Orchestra conducted by Peter Ernst Lassen.

results.creator_credit: Victor Vicas, International Film Associates and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: Portrays the kind of political and social chaos from which dictators draw their strength, and shows how military victories built Napoleon’s popularity.

results.title: Napoleon: the end of a dictator
results.creator_credit: Victor Vicas, International Film Associates and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: Explores the causes of the downfall of Napoleon, including the weariness caused by war, tyranny in France and the new nationalism throughout Europe.
